system.description: Treatises are encyclopedic works on a specific general proficiency such as Animal Husbandry, Healing, Knowledge (astrology), or Profession (lawyer). A typical treatise is a 12.5 high x 10 wide codex with 1,000 parchment or papyrus pages stitched into a leather- wrapped wood case. Treatises come in four tiers equivalent to proficiency ranks. An adventurer can only comprehend and benefit from a treatise of one rank higher than his own in the proficiency. It takes 6 days of dedicated activity for an adventurer to read a treatise. After reading the treatise, the adventurer can thereafter reference it to re-roll a proficiency throw for a particular activity or query. The re-roll is made at the rank of the adventurer or the treatise, whichever is worse. Referencing a treatise is an ancillary activity requiring 6 turns (one hour). The adventurer can also use the treatise to train or re-train that proficiency (p. XX) or to add its value to his research library (p. XX). See Axioms 14: Codex & Scroll for more rules on books.
